author: Jalal-ud-din, Jalal, Maulvi
title: Begam Sahiba ka Naya Chochla wa Char Sauton ki Larai
notes: Anti-polygamy satire ('the fight of four co-wives'). Date given only as June 1910.

author: Jauhar, M. S. Mr.
title: Jauhar Theatre
gloss: A collection of songs selected from various dramas.
publisher: Bhai Daya Singh and Son, Book-sellers
notes: Theatre songbook — commercial stage repertoire in print.

author: Jauhar, M. S. Mr.
title: Naghma-i-Sukhan
gloss: A collection of love verses from various poets.
publisher: Bhai Daya Singh and Son, Book-sellers
notes: Author dash-continuation of serial 49. [Previous edition noticed in entry No. 32 on page 55 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1908.]

author: Krishn Gopal, Diwan
title: Nawa-i-Nai
publisher: Author who is a Tahsildar in the Punjab
copyright: Diwan Krishn Gopal, Wazirabad; No. 12(?), dated the 13th August, 1910.
notes: Same Tahsildar-author as the novel at p28 s24 — Krishna devotional verse in Urdu.

author: Krishn Gopal, Diwan
title: Naz-o-Nayaz
gloss: Verses describing the love of Radha for Krishna.
publisher: Author who is a Tahsildar in the Punjab
copyright: Diwan Krishn Gopal, Wazirabad; No. 13(?), dated the 13th August, 1910.

author: Muhammad Husain, Azad, Maulavi
title: Nazm-i-Azad
gloss: Selections from the poems of the author on moral subjects, compiled by his son Agha Muhammad Ibrahim.
publisher: The compiler who is Munsif at Amritsar
notes: Muhammad Husain Azad (d. Jan 1910) — poems compiled by his son months after his death; canonical Urdu modernist.

title: Munajat
publisher: Shaikh Alah Bakhsh Ilahi Bakhsh, Book-sellers

author: Munir Hasan, Sayad
title: Sharab-khawaran ko notice
notes: Title mixes Urdu and English legalese ('notice' to wine-drinkers) — temperance verse.

author: Nawab Mirza Khan, Dagh
title: Mahtab-i-Dagh
publisher: Lala Sri Ram, M. A., Munsif
copyright: Lala Siri Ram, Delhi; No. 13(?), dated the (?) September, 1910.
notes: Posthumous divan of Dagh Dehlvi (d. 1905), published by Lala Sri Ram (compiler of Khumkhana-i-Javed) — Hindu munsif as custodian of the Urdu canon.

flags.issue: register number and day partially legible
